page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ine.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance based on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon Sapphire Rapids processor running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs, resulting in a thread-per-core ratio of 1.0. It includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for operating system and application data. It also lacks dedicated GPU hardware, making it unsuitable for hardware-accelerated graphics or deep learning workloads. For network connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent compute performance without resource contention from other tenants on the host. This resource profile offers a balanced entry-point configuration for workloads requiring predictable CPU execution. Appropriate use cases include lightweight web serving, microservices, development environments, and small-scale application hosting where a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio is optimal.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ine.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon Sapphire Rapids x86_64 processor operating at 2.7 GHz, featuring 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The instance provides 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage for data persistence. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps. With its dedicated CPU allocation, this instance delivers predictable, non-shared compute performance. It is designed for general-purpose workloads such as lightweight web serving, microservices, and development environments that require a balanced ratio of compute to memory without the need for local disk storage.
